channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Neanderthals may have drilled out a cavity 59,000 years ago
item.description: Scientists dug up a Paleolithic tooth that shows signs that these hominins may have been capable of executing a precise dental procedure.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists dug up a Paleolithic tooth that shows signs that these hominins may have been capable of executing a precise dental procedure.

(Image credit: Zubova et al.)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: 24 hours with 3 teenage birders: Welcome to the World Series of Birding
item.description: The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.
item.content:encoded:
The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.

(Image credit: Mohamed Sadek for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Escobar

item.title: 'We're dry:' The new U.S. Wildland Fire Service prepares for extreme fire season
item.description: Brian Fennessy, new head of the U.S. Wildland Fire Service, says his agency is 'trying to bring on additional aircraft and bring them on early,' and dismisses criticism of prevention methods.
item.content:encoded:
Brian Fennessy, new head of the U.S. Wildland Fire Service, says his agency is 'trying to bring on additional aircraft and bring them on early,' and dismisses criticism of prevention methods.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
item.description: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.
item.content:encoded:
Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Rooted in nature, 'Silent Friend' will change the way you see the trees
item.description: A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.
item.content:encoded:
A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.

(Image credit: Lena Kettner)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
item.description: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.content:encoded: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Studying these young Alzheimer's patients led to breakthroughs. Trump cut the funding
item.description: Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.
item.content:encoded:
Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.

(Image credit: Juan Diego Reyes for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: As federal scientists faced turmoil, the Devils Hole pupfish reached a crisis point
item.description: The Devils hole pupfish lives in just one spot in Death Valley. Wildlife officials have managed this iconic fish for decades, and last spring, just as the Trump administration was laying off all kinds of scientists, the wild population of this fish plummeted to only 20 individuals. Officials then took an irrevocable step.
item.content:encoded:
The Devils hole pupfish lives in just one spot in Death Valley. Wildlife officials have managed this iconic fish for decades, and last spring, just as the Trump administration was laying off all kinds of scientists, the wild population of this fish plummeted to only 20 individuals. Officials then took an irrevocable step.

(Image credit: Olin Feuerbacher)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
item.description: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.
item.content:encoded:
Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Katerina Barton

item.title: Making a podcast helped one family talk about aging, dementia and death
item.description: This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.
item.content:encoded:
This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.

(Image credit: Matthew Coughlin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny
